---
title: "LRPITT11: modified-ITT analysis of nonword reading (N) — floor-rule branch"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Substantially revised by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

**In plain terms.** Among children whose nonword-reading score was observed to be zero before randomisation, did those offered the intervention become more likely to score above zero at t2? Random assignment supports a causal arm contrast for this pre-treatment subgroup, but this particular subgroup estimand was selected post-hoc, unknown baseline-floor status excludes children from it, and the repository does not contain all randomised children.

LRPITT11 is an available-case, modified-ITT reanalysis of nonword reading (`nonword`) over the randomised t1 $\rightarrow$ t2 phase. At baseline, 36/50 observed scores (72%) were zero; at t2, 34/53 observed scores (about 64%) were zero. The transition estimand requires baseline floor status even though baseline is not a regression covariate.

::: {.callout-important title="Post-hoc floored-outcome estimand — exploratory headline"}

The floor rule and its 40% threshold were adopted after inspecting these outcome distributions and earlier model results. It is therefore **post-hoc and data-adaptive**, not pre-registered or prospectively specified. Applying the mechanical gate without arm labels reduces treatment-effect-driven selection, but does not change that evidential status. This model:

- uses an **age-only** predictor (no baseline regression term);
- reports $\Pr(\text{post} > 0 \mid \text{observed pre} = 0)$ among 36 eligible children (21 intervention, 15 control) as the exploratory headline. Baseline floor status is pre-randomisation, so conditioning on its observed value preserves the causal arm contrast for that subgroup, subject to missingness assumptions;
- explicitly excludes three children with an observed t2 score but missing t1 score from this transition estimand (one intervention, two control), rather than treating missing baselines as zeros; and
- retains the graded Beta-Binomial $\tau$ over all 53 observed t2 scores as a flagged, **detection-limited secondary**.

:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Analysis population and eligibility"}

The published trial randomised 57 children (29 intervention, 28 wait-list), whereas the archived repository dataset contains 54 (28 intervention, 26 wait-list); one archived control child has neither a t1 nor t2 nonword score. This is therefore not a full-randomised-cohort ITT analysis. Each fit writes `baseline_floor_eligibility.csv` and records the same arm-specific counts in `config.json`; the causal reading requires an explicit missingness assumption or a sensitivity analysis for unavailable randomised participants and unknown eligibility.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**; linear age is a precision term only.
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome — here, raises the probability of coming off
the floor.

:::

## Model

Exploratory headline (binary off-floor transition among children with observed $N_{\text{pre}}=0$), with $\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\,G_i + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i}$:

$$
[\,N_{\text{post},i} > 0\,] \sim \text{Bernoulli}\big(\sigma(\eta_i)\big).
$$

SECONDARY (graded, detection-limited): the *same* age-only $\eta_i$ with a
Beta-Binomial likelihood on the count, $N_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$, $N = 6$.

![Model diagram (exploratory off-floor analysis)](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_floored.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
